form_semantics.justification: Every input has a visible label, all sections use fieldset/legend including nested fieldsets for radio and checkbox sub-groups, appropriate input types used (text, textarea, file, checkbox, radio), autocomplete on display-name, no placeholder-only fields.

element_choice.justification: Toggle switches correctly use input[type='checkbox'] with CSS styling rather than custom div patterns, file upload uses input[type='file'], Delete Account is button[type='button'], Save Changes is button[type='submit'], textarea used for bio — every element is the best semantic match.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single h1 ('Account Settings') in main; fieldset legends serve as section headings without duplicating heading tags, which is correct per rubric guidance. No skipped heading levels.

aria_discipline.justification: The email-notifications checkbox carries aria-describedby='email-notifications-hint' but no element with that id exists (the hint span has only a class, not an id), creating a dangling reference that silently fails for screen readers. All other ARIA usage is disciplined and appropriate.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link, header, and main with id are all present and correct. No footer, but the page is simple enough that its absence is not a significant gap. No unnecessary landmark overuse.

content_realism.justification: Realistic field labels, descriptive hint text, plausible accept attributes for the file input, and meaningful radio/checkbox values — no placeholder or Lorem ipsum content.

expectations_met: Radio button groups are within a <fieldset> with a <legend> describing the group
Toggle switches use checkbox inputs (not custom div patterns)
File upload uses <input type='file'> with a label
'Delete Account' is a <button> — not a link
Legends serve as visual section headings (no redundant heading + legend pairs)
Radio buttons have matching name attributes within their group
The form as a whole is a <form> element
expectations_missed: aria-describedby='email-notifications-hint' on the email toggle references a non-existent id — the hint <span> has no id attribute, so the association is broken
additional_observations: Nested fieldsets for notification frequency and profile visibility sub-groups show strong form grouping discipline
The form uses aria-labelledby pointing to a hidden span with 'Account Settings' text — slightly redundant with the page h1 but not harmful
tabindex='-1' on <main> correctly supports the skip link target
